# Procedure — DEL-019-04: Construction Quality Control

**Document type:** Procedure (operational)
**Deliverable ID:** DEL-019-04_QC-Management
**Generated by:** 4_DOCUMENTS Pass 1+2; enriched Pass 3
**Date:** 2026-02-26

---

## Purpose

This Procedure describes the steps to establish and operate the construction quality control (QC) program for the WDMLRL Maintenance Shop Addition (AB-2026-01424). It covers program setup (QC Plan, schedule, tracking systems) and ongoing operation (inspections, testing, deficiency management, reporting) through to construction completion.

---

## Prerequisites

### Upstream deliverable dependencies (declared)

| Prerequisite | Status | Source |
|---|---|---|
| DEL-019-01 (Prime-Contractor Designation & OH&S Program) — must be established before QC authority structure can be confirmed | Required upstream — see _DEPENDENCIES.md | _DEPENDENCIES.md |
| DEL-019-02 (Weekly-Coordination) — meeting cadence must be active before QC status reporting begins | Required upstream | _DEPENDENCIES.md |

### Personnel

| Prerequisite | Status |
|---|---|
| QC Manager assigned, confirmed by name, and meeting minimum qualification requirements (see REQ-QC-11) | Outstanding — see _STATUS.md |
| Project Manager confirmed | Per _CONTEXT.md — Key Roles |
| Inspection personnel competency verified (see REQ-QC-18) | Required before independent inspections |

### Documentation prerequisites

| Prerequisite | Status | Source |
|---|---|---|
| IFC drawings available (current revision) for construction phase being inspected | Required per REQ-QC-09 | RFP §3.3.2 |
| CCDC 14-2013 contract executed | Required before construction commencement | RFP §2.7 |
| Prime Contractor Designation form completed and filed with County | Required per RFP §2.15 | RFP §2.15 |

### Reference materials

- RFP §1.0, §2.10, §2.11, §2.14, §2.15, §3.3.2, §4.7.5 (AB-2026-01424-WDMLRL RFP.pdf)
- DEL-019-01 OH&S Program (for role and escalation alignment)
- Current IFC drawings (all disciplines) for construction phase in progress
- Applicable test method standards (ASTM, CSA — specific standards TBD per REQ-QC-14)

---

## Steps

### Phase A — Program Establishment (Pre-Construction)

**A1. Assign QC Manager**

Confirm and document the QC Manager by name. Verify the QC Manager meets minimum qualification requirements as defined in the QC Plan (see REQ-QC-11). Communicate the assignment to the County project manager and record in the QC Plan.

**Responsible party:** Project Manager
**Output:** Named QC Manager on record; qualification verification on file.
**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Key Roles; _STATUS.md — Outstanding Items (REQ-QC-11); Pass 3 enrichment B-003.

---

**A2. Draft QC Plan**

Develop a written QC Plan covering (minimum content per REQ-QC-01):
- Project QC objectives and scope.
- Roles and responsibilities (Project Manager, QC Manager, subcontractor QC responsibilities).
- Inspection hold-point structure aligned with construction phases.
- Material quality control procedures (receipt verification, testing requirements by material type).
- Deficiency identification, documentation, severity classification (critical/major/minor per REQ-QC-06), assignment, and close-out process with differentiated response timelines per severity category.
- Communication processes for deficiency correction (how deficiencies are reported, to whom, within what timeframe).
- Reporting cadence and format for weekly coordination meetings (DEL-019-02), including KPI reporting (per REQ-QC-15).
- How the QC program extends to subcontractors (equivalent inspection rigor per REQ-QC-07).
- QC Plan approval authority and approval chain (per REQ-QC-13).
- QC performance metrics and KPI targets (per REQ-QC-15).
- Conformance determination process for construction completion (per REQ-QC-16).
- QC Manager qualification requirements (per REQ-QC-11).
- Inspection personnel competency requirements (per REQ-QC-18).
- County notification lead time for inspections (recommended minimum 48 hours — see Guidance Trade-offs).
- Standard inspection report template (see Guidance — Standardized inspection report format).
- QC program onboarding requirements for new personnel and subcontractors (see Guidance — QC program onboarding and training).
- QC dispute resolution and escalation process (see Guidance — QC dispute resolution and escalation).
- Record retention format and storage location (per Procedure C4).
- Third-party testing laboratory qualification requirements, if applicable (per REQ-QC-17).

Ensure internal consistency with DEL-019-01 OH&S Program (roles, escalation paths).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager (draft), Project Manager (review and approval submission).
**Output:** Draft QC Plan document.
**Basis:** RFP §4.7.5; _CONTEXT.md — Scope Definition; REQ-QC-01; Pass 3 enrichments F-001, A-001, A-004, B-003, C-002, D-001, D-002, E-002, E-003, F-002, X-001, X-002.

---

**A3. Obtain QC Plan Approval**

Submit the QC Plan for approval per the approval chain defined in the QC Plan (REQ-QC-13). The Plan must be documented and approved before construction activities begin.

**Responsible party:** Project Manager.
**Output:** Approved QC Plan on file with approval signatures.
**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria; REQ-QC-01, REQ-QC-13.
**Note:** Approval authority (internal, County, or both) is TBD — see Conflict Table CONF-002 in Guidance.md; the QC Plan must define this before submission.

---

**A4. Develop Inspection Schedule**

Prepare an inspection schedule that:
- Identifies inspection hold points for each significant construction phase (e.g., pre-pour concrete, structural connections, pre-close rough-ins for mechanical/electrical/plumbing, building envelope, final punch-list).
- Is aligned with the overall project construction schedule (see DEL-019-02 for schedule context).
- Includes mandatory notification to County project representative for each inspection point with defined lead time (minimum 48 hours recommended — see Guidance Trade-offs; confirmed lead time per QC Plan).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager (draft), Project Manager (review).
**Output:** Inspection Schedule document.
**Basis:** RFP §3.3.2; _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria; REQ-QC-02.

---

**A5. Establish Deficiency Tracking System**

Set up a deficiency log or tracking register. The register must, at minimum, capture:
- Deficiency ID and date identified.
- Severity classification (critical / major / minor) per the scheme defined in the QC Plan (REQ-QC-06).
- Description and location of deficiency.
- Responsible party for remediation.
- Target remediation date (differentiated by severity category).
- Remediation status (open/in progress/completed).
- Verification date and verifier sign-off.
- Escalation flag (if deficiency remediation is disputed — see Guidance, QC dispute resolution).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager.
**Output:** Deficiency log/register (format TBD; must support severity classification and differentiated response tracking).
**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Outstanding Items; REQ-QC-06; Pass 3 enrichment E-003.

---

**A6. Establish Testing Protocol Documents**

For each material or system requiring testing (e.g., concrete compressive strength, soil compaction, electrical continuity — specific protocols TBD based on IFC specifications), document:
- Test method, referencing the applicable test method standard (e.g., ASTM, CSA — per REQ-QC-14).
- Frequency or trigger for testing.
- Acceptance criteria (pass/fail thresholds per REQ-QC-05).
- Recording format.

**Responsible party:** QC Manager.
**Output:** Testing Protocol document(s) with referenced test method standards and defined acceptance criteria.
**Basis:** _CONTEXT.md — Success Criteria; REQ-QC-05, REQ-QC-14; Pass 3 enrichments B-001, B-002.
**Note:** Specific materials and systems requiring testing are TBD pending IFC specification completion.

---

**A7. Verify Inspection Personnel Competency (Pass 3 — F-002)**

Before any inspection personnel (QC Manager or delegates) conduct inspections independently, verify their competency for the specific inspection types they will perform. Document the verification.

**Responsible party:** Project Manager (verification); QC Manager (support).
**Output:** Competency verification records on file.
**Basis:** REQ-QC-18; Pass 3 enrichment F-002.
**Note:** Minimum competency criteria to be defined in the QC Plan.

---

**A8. Conduct QC Program Onboarding (Pass 3 — E-002)**

Before construction begins, orient all relevant personnel (QC Manager, inspection delegates, subcontractor foremen) on the QC procedures and expectations. Document the onboarding. Repeat onboarding for new personnel or subcontractors joining during construction.

**Responsible party:** QC Manager.
**Output:** Onboarding attendance records.
**Basis:** ASSUMPTION: standard construction QC practice; Guidance — QC program onboarding and training; Pass 3 enrichment E-002.

---

**A9. Qualify Third-Party Testing Laboratories (Pass 3 — X-005)**

If third-party testing laboratories will be used, verify that each laboratory holds applicable accreditation (e.g., ISO 17025 or equivalent) for the test methods to be performed. Obtain and file accreditation certificates.

**Responsible party:** QC Manager.
**Output:** Third-party lab accreditation certificates on file.
**Basis:** REQ-QC-17; Pass 3 enrichment X-005.
**Note:** This step applies only if third-party testing is required. Determination TBD pending IFC specification completion.

---

### Phase B — Ongoing QC Operations (During Construction)

**B1. Notify County Representative of Upcoming Inspections**

Before each inspection hold point, notify the County project representative with the minimum lead time defined in the QC Plan (recommended minimum 48 hours — see Guidance Trade-offs; confirmed lead time per QC Plan).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager.
**Basis:** RFP §3.3.2; REQ-QC-03; Pass 3 enrichment X-001.
**Note (Pass 3 — X-001):** The minimum notification lead time is TBD in accessible sources. The QC Plan shall define a specific lead time based on County PM input. A default of 48 hours minimum is proposed (see Guidance Trade-offs).

---

**B2. Submit Inspection Request**

Submit the formal inspection request for each inspection event.

**Responsible party:** QC Manager.
**Output:** Inspection request log entry.
**Basis:** RFP §3.3.2; REQ-QC-04.
**Note:** Submit to whom (County, Safety Codes authority, or both) is subject to Conflict CONF-001 in Guidance.md — pending human ruling.

---

**B3. Conduct Inspection**

At each inspection hold point:
1. Confirm County project representative is present (mandatory per REQ-QC-03).
2. Reference current IFC drawing revision for conformance check (REQ-QC-09).
3. Inspect work against IFC drawings and QC Plan requirements.
4. Complete the standardized inspection report form (per template defined in QC Plan — see Guidance, Standardized inspection report format).
5. Identify any non-conformances or deficiencies; classify by severity (REQ-QC-06).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager (conduct); County representative (attend).
**Output:** Completed inspection report using standardized template.
**Basis:** RFP §3.3.2; REQ-QC-02, REQ-QC-03, REQ-QC-09; Pass 3 enrichment C-002.

---

**B4. Issue Inspection Report to County**

Provide a copy of each completed inspection report to the County project representative.

**Responsible party:** QC Manager.
**Basis:** RFP §3.3.2; REQ-QC-03.

---

**B5. Execute Testing Protocols**

At the frequency or trigger defined in the Testing Protocol documents (Step A6), conduct required materials and systems tests. Record results and evaluate against defined acceptance criteria (REQ-QC-05). Reference the applicable test method standard for each test (REQ-QC-14).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager (coordinate); testing may be performed by QC Manager or qualified third-party lab (accredited per REQ-QC-17, if applicable).
**Output:** Completed test result records with pass/fail evaluation against acceptance criteria.
**Basis:** REQ-QC-05, REQ-QC-14, REQ-QC-17; Pass 3 enrichments B-001, B-002, X-005.

---

**B6. Log and Assign Deficiencies**

Any deficiency identified during inspection or testing shall be immediately entered into the Deficiency Log (Step A5). Classify by severity (critical/major/minor per REQ-QC-06). Assign responsible party and target remediation date (differentiated by severity category).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager.
**Basis:** REQ-QC-06; Pass 3 enrichment E-003.

---

**B7. Verify Deficiency Remediation**

Upon notification that a deficiency has been remediated:
1. Re-inspect the remediated work.
2. If satisfactory, close the deficiency in the log with date and verifier sign-off.
3. If unsatisfactory, reopen and re-assign.
4. If the remediation adequacy is disputed, follow the escalation process defined in the QC Plan (see Guidance — QC dispute resolution and escalation).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager.
**Output:** Closed deficiency record with sign-off; or escalation record if disputed.
**Basis:** RFP §2.10.4; REQ-QC-06; Pass 3 enrichment D-002.

---

**B8. Subcontractor QC Oversight**

Apply QC inspection and deficiency-tracking procedures to subcontractor work products with the same inspection and sign-off rigor as direct Proponent work (REQ-QC-07). Document subcontractor-related deficiencies in the Deficiency Log with severity classification. Report persistent subcontractor quality issues to the Project Manager for action under DEL-019-03 (Subcontractor Management).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager (day-to-day); Project Manager (escalation).
**Basis:** RFP §2.15; REQ-QC-07; _DEPENDENCIES.md; Pass 3 enrichment C-001.

---

**B9. Prepare QC Status Report for Weekly Meeting**

Before each weekly coordination meeting (DEL-019-02), compile a QC status summary including:
- Inspections completed since last meeting and key findings.
- Upcoming inspection hold points.
- Open deficiencies by severity category (count, ages, responsible parties).
- Deficiencies closed since last meeting.
- Testing results received since last meeting.
- QC performance metrics against KPI targets (REQ-QC-15).

**Responsible party:** QC Manager (compile); Project Manager (present).
**Output:** QC status agenda item for weekly meeting minutes.
**Basis:** _DEPENDENCIES.md; REQ-QC-08, REQ-QC-15; Pass 3 enrichment A-004.

---

**B10. Conduct Periodic QC Program Self-Assessment (Pass 3 — E-004)**

At a defined periodic interval (recommended monthly — frequency to be confirmed in QC Plan), conduct an internal self-assessment of the QC program to verify it is being executed as designed. The self-assessment should check:
- Inspection schedule adherence (inspections conducted per schedule).
- Deficiency log completeness (all deficiencies logged, classified, tracked).
- County notification and attendance compliance.
- Testing protocol execution (tests conducted per frequency, results recorded, evaluated against acceptance criteria).
- Record completeness and filing.
- KPI performance against targets.

Document findings and any corrective actions required.

**Responsible party:** QC Manager (conduct); Project Manager (review findings).
**Output:** QC Program Self-Assessment Report.
**Basis:** ASSUMPTION: standard QC practice includes programmatic self-assessment; REQ-QC-15 (performance metrics imply periodic measurement); Pass 3 enrichment E-004.

---

### Phase C — Construction Completion

**C1. Final Punch-List Inspection**

Before requesting CCC, the Project Manager shall satisfy themselves that all work is complete, all deficiencies are closed (zero open items in Deficiency Log across all severity categories), and the Works are in a clean and tidy condition ready for acceptance by the Owner.

**Responsible party:** Project Manager; QC Manager.
**Basis:** RFP §2.14.1; REQ-QC-06.

---

**C2. Render Conformance Determination (Pass 3 — D-001)**

Execute the formal conformance determination process defined in the QC Plan (REQ-QC-16). Document the final pass/fail verdict on overall QC program completion, including:
- Confirmation that all requirements (REQ-QC-01 through REQ-QC-18) have been satisfied.
- Identification of the authority rendering the verdict.
- Resolution of any remaining disputed findings.

**Responsible party:** Per QC Plan (REQ-QC-16).
**Output:** Documented conformance determination.
**Basis:** REQ-QC-16; RFP §2.14.1; Pass 3 enrichment D-001.

---

**C3. Request Final Inspection**

Submit a written request to the Owner to conduct a final inspection of the Works.

**Responsible party:** Project Manager.
**Basis:** RFP §2.14.2.

---

**C4. Support CCC Process**

Support the Owner's final inspection and provide all QC documentation (inspection reports, test results, deficiency log — fully closed, conformance determination, KPI performance summary) required for the Owner to issue the Construction Completion Certificate.

**Responsible party:** Project Manager; QC Manager.
**Basis:** RFP §2.14.3.

---

**C5. Retain QC Records**

Retain all QC records (inspection reports, test results, deficiency log, conformance determination, self-assessment reports, personnel competency records, third-party lab accreditation certificates) for the duration of the guarantee period (construction period + 2 years post-CCC). Records shall be stored in the format and location defined in the QC Plan (minimum requirement: digital backup to ensure retrievability during guarantee period).

**Responsible party:** Project Manager.
**Basis:** RFP §2.10.2; Pass 3 enrichment X-002.
**Note (Pass 3 — X-002):** Specific retention format and storage location are TBD — to be defined in the QC Plan. At minimum, digital backup is required to ensure records remain accessible during the guarantee period.

---

## Verification

| Step | Verification Check |
|---|---|
| A1 | QC Manager name is documented; qualification requirements met and verified; County notified |
| A2-A3 | Approved QC Plan on file before construction commences; minimum content per REQ-QC-01 confirmed |
| A4 | Inspection Schedule on file and consistent with construction schedule; County notification lead time defined |
| A5 | Deficiency Log system operational with severity classification capability before first inspection |
| A6 | Testing Protocol documents on file with acceptance criteria and test method standard references before relevant construction phase begins |
| A7 | Inspection personnel competency verified and documented before independent inspections |
| A8 | QC onboarding completed for all relevant personnel before construction begins |
| A9 | Third-party lab accreditation certificates on file (if applicable) |
| B1-B4 | Each inspection report shows County representative attendance; standardized template used; copy filed |
| B5 | Test result records filed per protocol frequency; results evaluated against acceptance criteria |
| B6-B7 | No deficiency remains open in log without severity classification, assigned responsible party, and target date; disputed items follow escalation process |
| B8 | Subcontractor deficiencies tracked with same rigor as Proponent work; cross-referenced to DEL-019-03 as applicable |
| B9 | QC status with KPI performance appears in weekly meeting minutes |
| B10 | Periodic self-assessment reports on file; corrective actions documented |
| C1 | Deficiency Log shows zero open items across all severity categories before CCC request |
| C2 | Conformance determination documented with authority identified |
| C3 | Written CCC request on file |
| C4 | Full QC documentation package assembled and available for Owner review |
| C5 | Records archived in defined format with guaranteed retrieval for guarantee period |

---

## Records

The following records shall result from execution of this Procedure:

| Record | Responsible Custodian | Retention |
|---|---|---|
| Approved QC Plan | Project Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| Inspection Schedule | QC Manager | Project duration |
| Inspection Request Log | QC Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| Inspection Reports (all — standardized template) | QC Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| Testing Protocol Documents (with test method standards and acceptance criteria) | QC Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| Test Result Records (with pass/fail evaluation) | QC Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| Deficiency Log (complete history with severity classification) | QC Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| Deficiency Close-Out Sign-Off Records | QC Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| QC Status Report summaries with KPI performance (via weekly meeting minutes) | Project Manager | Project duration |
| QC Manager Assignment and Qualification Record | Project Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| Inspection Personnel Competency Verification Records | Project Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| QC Program Self-Assessment Reports | QC Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| QC Program Onboarding Attendance Records | QC Manager | Project duration |
| Third-Party Lab Accreditation Certificates (if applicable) | QC Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| Conformance Determination Record | Project Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |
| QC Dispute Escalation Records (if any) | Project Manager | Project duration + guarantee period |

**Note:** "Guarantee period" = construction period + 2 years post-CCC (RFP §2.10.2). Record retention format: TBD — to be defined in QC Plan; minimum requirement is digital backup to ensure retrievability (Pass 3 — X-002).
